authoritative_sources.title: 2022 NAICS Sector 51 - Information
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Census Bureau publishes the official NAICS classification used to define the Information sector, grounding the industry overlay in software, hosting, data, web portal, publishing, and media-service contexts.
used_for: information-sector scope
software, data, hosting, web, publishing, and media terminology
avoiding generic IT support framing
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Keeps the lane anchored to information, software, and digital media platform workflows rather than cross-industry operations language.

authoritative_sources.title: ISO/IEC 20000-1:2018 Information technology - Service management - Part 1: Service management system requirements
authoritative_sources.publisher: International Organization for Standardization and International Electrotechnical Commission
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISO/IEC 20000-1 is a recognized service management system standard for technology service planning, delivery, monitoring, review, and improvement, directly grounding platform operations workflow and evidence discipline.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Service intake, incident and request handling, service-level handling, operation, review, and improvement
used_for: service request, incident, problem, change, and lifecycle vocabulary
service evidence and completion discipline
review, monitoring, and improvement handoff boundaries
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires platform state transitions to be tied to documented service records, owners, and evidence rather than informal queue movement.

authoritative_sources.title: ITIL service management framework
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PeopleCert and AXELOS steward ITIL and publish public practice groupings for monitoring, support, fulfillment, change enablement, deployment, release, configuration, supplier, service-level, and information-security management.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Service desk classification, incident/request/problem distinction, change enablement, deployment, release, configuration, monitoring, and escalation
used_for: separating incidents, service requests, problems, changes, releases, and monitoring events
change-readiness and release handoff behavior
handoff to engineering, security, product, delivery, and incident owners
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane to platform operations work packages and owner routing instead of unowned production action.

authoritative_sources.title: Site Reliability Engineering
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Google's public SRE book is a recognized operating reference for reliability practices in software platforms, including SLOs, monitoring, incident response, change management, and post-incident learning.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Reliability triage, SLO or SLA impact framing, monitoring evidence, incident communication, and post-incident review
used_for: SLO, error budget, monitoring, status, incident, and post-incident terminology
impact and recovery evidence expectations
boundary between operational triage and engineering ownership
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Adds platform-specific reliability language and requires status summaries to separate observed evidence from engineering decisions.

authoritative_sources.title: Incident Response Recommendations and Considerations for Cybersecurity Risk Management
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 800-61 Rev. 3 is official U.S. public guidance for incident response preparation, detection, analysis, containment, recovery, communication, and lessons learned, supporting incident lifecycle and evidence expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Incident preparation, detection, analysis, response coordination, recovery, communication, and closeout evidence
used_for: incident lifecycle states and severity-aware triage
impact, owner, containment, recovery, communication, and lessons-learned evidence
routing when an incident crosses security, engineering, or executive authority
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Defines why incident closure requires evidence of impact, owner action, validation, communication, and follow-up rather than unsupported closure.

authoritative_sources.title: Guide for Security-Focused Configuration Management of Information Systems
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 800-128 is official public guidance for configuration management planning, change control, impact analysis, monitoring, and evidence needed before production technology changes are treated as ready.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Change-readiness, configuration impact analysis, testing, approval, rollback, monitoring, and post-change evidence
used_for: change request prerequisite validation
testing, rollback, approval, monitoring, and configuration evidence
refusal triggers for unapproved production changes
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Makes missing test, rollback, impact, or approval evidence a blocker for platform configuration, connector, feature-flag, content workflow, and integration changes.

authoritative_sources.title: Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5 provides recognized public controls for access control, configuration management, audit logging, incident response, system integrity, and privacy controls that limit autonomous access, change, and confidentiality handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Access-readiness, audit trail, configuration control, confidentiality, and privacy-control boundary handling
used_for: access request evidence and least-privilege owner routing
audit log and retention expectations
confidentiality and security exception refusal triggers
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from granting access or overriding security controls while allowing evidence-backed access-readiness packaging.

authoritative_sources.title: NIST Privacy Framework
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST's Privacy Framework is an official privacy-risk management framework used to define privacy and confidentiality constraints when platform operations handles customer, subscriber, client, or tenant data.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Confidentiality screening, privacy-sensitive routing, data-minimization, and approved disclosure
used_for: customer, subscriber, client, and tenant data handling constraints
privacy-rights escalation triggers
recipient, channel, and disclosure refusal behavior
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires subscriber and client data requests to be handled through tenant-approved purpose, recipient, channel, and authority checks.

authoritative_sources.title: Protecting Personal Information: A Guide for Business
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The FTC is the U.S. consumer-protection regulator and its business guidance is authoritative for personal-information protection expectations relevant to platform operations access, disclosure, retention, and escalation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Customer data handling, controlled communication, and unsafe disclosure refusal
used_for: data minimization and secure handling expectations
unapproved recipient or channel refusal triggers
customer harm escalation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Supports refusal when raw logs, subscriber records, or personal information would be disclosed outside approved controls.

authoritative_sources.title: Trust Services Criteria for Security, Availability, Processing Integrity, Confidentiality, and Privacy
authoritative_sources.publisher: American Institute of Certified Public Accountants
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AICPA trust services criteria are a recognized service-organization control framework for security, availability, processing integrity, confidentiality, and privacy, supporting platform-operations caveats around availability, data handling, and evidence claims.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Availability, confidentiality, privacy, processing-integrity, and trust-claim boundary handling
used_for: availability and processing-integrity claim caveats
control-evidence and confidentiality boundaries
SOC-style trust claim escalation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents platform operations from making attestation or trust-service conclusions while preserving evidence for qualified reviewers.

authoritative_sources.title: Section 512 of Title 17: Resources on Online Service Provider Safe Harbors and Notice-and-Takedown
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The U.S. Copyright Office provides official resources on online service provider notice-and-takedown and counter-notice processes, making it authoritative for digital media content workflow boundaries in platform operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Content workflow status handling, takedown or counter-notice boundary screening, and legal-owner handoff
used_for: content removal and counter-notice escalation
legal versus operations-lane boundary
content asset evidence and approval requirements
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Allows the lane to summarize content workflow state and route evidence while refusing legal or editorial decisions.

workflow_stages.name: Platform operations intake and authority prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm tenant service model, client or product scope, platform, environment, affected workflow, content lifecycle state, work type, incident severity, access owner, change owner, approval rules, confidentiality constraints, and systems of record before accepting execution.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Truth-set assembly for tickets, systems, interfaces, and client artifacts
workflow_stages.description: Assemble tickets, engagement records, logs, tenant records, content records, runbooks, release records, approval records, client communications, and retrieval locations into an auditable evidence set.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Incident, access, and exception triage
workflow_stages.description: Classify incidents, access requests, job failures, API errors, connector failures, content workflow blockers, observability conflicts, source conflicts, and confidentiality conflicts into lifecycle states, owners, impact, and next actions without claiming unowned authority.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Change-readiness and integration-risk handling
workflow_stages.description: Validate change ticket, affected systems, field mapping, test evidence, rollback plan, monitoring plan, change window, approvals, client impact, content impact, and data-flow constraints before treating a platform, connector, feature-flag, workflow, or content change as ready.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Client, content, confidentiality, and approval-aware delivery
workflow_stages.description: Apply engagement scope, approved recipient, approved delivery channel, client communication, subscriber-data, operational-log, platform-credential, unpublished-content, and content approval checks before producing or sending operational outputs.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Validation, completion evidence, and closeout
workflow_stages.description: Capture what changed or recovered, who owned it, what evidence validates the outcome, what client communication occurred, what rollback or follow-up remains, and where the completion record is retained before marking work complete.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

artifacts: Tenant operating policy, service model, support tier, approval matrix, escalation threshold, confidentiality rules, quality standards, status communication rules, change window rules, and rollback expectations

Client engagement scope, statement of work, contract references, client commitments, business unit, product, subscriber, account, workspace, content collection, jurisdiction, and approved communication channels
Project plan, ticket, incident report, access request, change request, runbook, release note, QA checklist, owner map, reviewer map, and approval records

Project management, CRM, ticketing, document management, content management, delivery, entitlement, identity, source control, release, configuration, observability, status-page, and audit-log references

Tenant IDs, workspace IDs, content IDs, asset metadata, entitlement records, feature flags, API endpoints, webhook payloads, event streams, batch-job runs, connector mappings, deployment records, service accounts, and event logs

Access-entitlement evidence, service-account or API-key owner record, least-privilege justification, security-review record, monitoring alert, incident severity record, impact summary, containment or recovery note, and validation evidence

Test results, retest evidence, rollback plan, change approval, implementation record, post-change monitoring evidence, client communication log, exception queue note, escalation note, completion record, and retention location
systems_of_record: ticketing, service desk, incident, problem, and change management system
document management and client file system
content management, digital asset management, publishing, moderation, review, and localization systems
subscription, entitlement, account, billing, identity, and tenant administration systems
source control, CI/CD, release, configuration, feature-flag, deployment, and rollback systems
API gateway, integration platform, event stream, webhook, batch job, connector, and partner systems
monitoring, observability, logging, tracing, status-page, and incident communication systems
identity, access, service-account, API-key, and secrets-management systems
client communication, approval, secure-delivery, audit-log, and retention systems

allowed: Determine whether documented platform operations evidence supports moving work to accepted intake, investigating, awaiting evidence, ready for change, scheduled, blocked, rework, escalated, implemented-by-owner, validating, completed, or closed.

Prepare evidence-backed execution status updates, incident summaries, access-readiness notes, change-readiness packages, integration-risk escalations, exception queue notes, and completion evidence records.

Normalize platform operations terms and artifacts such as tenant, workspace, entitlement, service request, incident, problem, change record, feature flag, API endpoint, webhook payload, batch run, SLO, status page, content ID, approval record, and audit log into lifecycle state and owner routing.
escalate: The applicable tenant policy, platform policy, client scope, jurisdiction, contract term, approval owner, security control, privacy obligation, content workflow rule, or system record is missing and would change the workflow.
Ticket, logs, client records, platform records, content records, entitlement records, observability data, access records, test results, rollback plans, or approval evidence disagree materially.

Legal claims, editorial approval, privacy-rights handling, copyright or takedown workflow, access-control exception, production engineering decision, client rights, or professional judgment determines the answer.

Incident impact, service credits, delivery commitments, contractual obligations, security exceptions, privacy posture, attestation claims, or final client acceptance requires account, finance, legal, privacy, security, engineering, quality, or executive approval.

A confidentiality, approved-recipient, approved-channel, subscriber-data, platform-credential, operational-log, unpublished-content, client-rights, or data-security conflict affects delivery or use of information.
refuse: Requests to approve legal advice, privacy-law interpretation, editorial content, copyright or takedown outcome, security policy exception, production engineering change, financial credit, contractual exception, attestation conclusion, or final client acceptance.

Requests to grant access, approve a change, launch an integration, publish content, mark an incident resolved, or close work without required ticket, owner approval, test, rollback, validation, client communication, content approval, audit, or retention evidence.

Requests to suppress exceptions, bypass access controls, skip rollback planning, ignore conflicting source records, disclose restricted client data, or treat a tenant-specific assumption as established fact.

domain_failure_modes: A tenant, workspace, subscription, entitlement, content ID, API endpoint, webhook payload, event stream, batch run, feature flag, deployment record, approval record, or audit log is missing, stale, malformed, or unsupported by system evidence.
Incident triage proceeds without severity, affected system, client impact, owner, containment, validation, communication, or closeout evidence.
Access-readiness packaging is confused with access approval, allowing unreviewed console, API key, service account, partner portal, or content-system privileges.
Change-readiness is claimed without impact analysis, testing, rollback plan, monitoring, implementation owner, approval evidence, or change window.
SLO, SLA, availability, processing-integrity, content status, or root-cause statements are summarized as final conclusions without source evidence or specialist review.

Client files, subscriber data, operational logs, platform credentials, unpublished content, or performance records are disclosed through an unapproved recipient, channel, purpose, or engagement scope.

Client instructions, tenant runbook, vendor guidance, public standard, contract term, security control, privacy obligation, and content workflow rule conflict and the lane synthesizes a confident answer instead of escalating.
The lane drifts into legal, editorial, security, engineering, privacy, financial, contractual, attestation, or final client signoff authority because the work sits in an execution queue.

scenario_family_coverage.notes: Scenarios block work when tickets, logs, approvals, tests, rollback plans, source records, or authority-source context are missing.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Client request that crosses into advisory or signoff authority
scenario_family_coverage.notes: Scenarios route legal, editorial, privacy, security, engineering, financial, contractual, attestation, and final approval decisions.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Confidentiality or approval conflict in delivery workflow
scenario_family_coverage.notes: Scenarios enforce approved recipient, channel, engagement scope, testing, rollback, and approval evidence before delivery or change progression.
Queue-level must-pass family covered by blocked intake, webhook or content queue rework, and low-confidence missing-record handling.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
scenario_family_coverage.notes: Queue-level must-pass family covered by platform workflow, identity dependency, and conflicting-policy exceptions.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion
scenario_family_coverage.notes: Queue-level must-pass family covered by refusal to close change work without test, rollback, approval, validation, and retention evidence.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: A system dependency fails and cross-team routing is required
scenario_family_coverage.notes: Queue-level must-pass family covered by identity-provider outage with platform, security, engineering, delivery, and account routing.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: A requested change lacks testing, rollback, or approval evidence
scenario_family_coverage.notes: Queue-level must-pass family covered by webhook schema and connector remapping change-readiness hold.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Platform-specific role names and console permissions
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Public controls define access-control posture, but exact role names, service account scopes, and approval owners are tenant-specific and must be retrieved at runtime before acting.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific privacy, copyright, and digital media obligations
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Public regulator and framework sources define boundary triggers, but the lane must route legal, privacy-law, copyright, editorial, or content-rights interpretation to the appropriate owner when jurisdiction or tenant policy changes the result.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Client contract remedies and service credits for platform incidents
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Public service-management and trust frameworks support operational evidence handling, but actual service credits, remedies, contractual obligations, or client acceptance remain client-contract and account, finance, or legal authority.

notes: Research satisfied the professional-and-information-services domain pack by covering professional standards, client-delivery workflow, and confidentiality or quality-review references.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack because tenant-specific items are recorded as runtime prerequisites and refusal or escalation triggers.
The pack constrains the final spec and evaluation scenarios around incident, access, change-readiness, integration, content workflow, confidentiality, approval, and completion-evidence behavior.
